```python
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import anndata as AnnData
import liana as li
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Set up scanpy settings
sc.settings.verbosity = 3
sc.settings.set_figure_params(dpi=80, facecolor='white')

# Load the spatial transcriptomics data
print("Loading spatial transcriptomics data...")
adata = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/breast_cancer/breast_cancer_no_celltype.h5ad')
print(f"Loaded data shape: {adata.shape}")

# Load reference data
print("Loading reference data...")
ref_adata = sc.read_h5ad('./breast_cancer_reference.h5ad')
print(f"Reference data shape: {ref_adata.shape}")
print(f"Reference cell types: {ref_adata.obs['celltype'].unique()}")

# Make variable names unique
adata.var_names_make_unique()
ref_adata.var_names_make_unique()

# Find common genes between datasets
common_genes = adata.var_names.intersection(ref_adata.var_names)
print(f"Number of common genes: {len(common_genes)}")

# Subset both datasets to common genes
adata = adata[:, common_genes].copy()
ref_adata = ref_adata[:, common_genes].copy()

# Preprocess the spatial data
print("Preprocessing spatial data...")
sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata)
adata.raw = adata
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5)
adata = adata[:, adata.var.highly_variable]

# Preprocess reference data
print("Preprocessing reference data...")
ref_adata.raw = ref_adata
sc.pp.normalize_total(ref_adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(ref_adata)

# Find highly variable genes in reference
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(ref_adata, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5)

# Use intersection of highly variable genes
hvg_intersection = adata.var_names.intersection(ref_adata.var[ref_adata.var.highly_variable].index)
print(f"Number of highly variable genes for integration: {len(hvg_intersection)}")

adata = adata[:, hvg_intersection].copy()
ref_adata = ref_adata[:, hvg_intersection].copy()

# Scale data
sc.pp.scale(adata, max_value=10)
sc.pp.scale(ref_adata, max_value=10)

# PCA
sc.tl.pca(adata, svd_solver='arpack')
sc.tl.pca(ref_adata, svd_solver='arpack')

# Concatenate datasets for label transfer
print("Preparing data for label transfer...")
ref_adata.obs['batch'] = 'reference'
adata.obs['batch'] = 'query'
ref_adata.obs['celltype_original'] = ref_adata.obs['celltype'].copy()

# Create combined dataset
adata_combined = AnnData.concatenate(ref_adata, adata, batch_key='dataset', batch_categories=['reference', 'query'])

# Compute neighbors and UMAP for combined dataset
print("Computing neighborhood graph...")
sc.pp.neighbors(adata_combined, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=50)
sc.tl.umap(adata_combined)

# Perform label transfer using ingest
print("Performing label transfer...")
# Split back
ref_indices = adata_combined.obs['dataset'] == 'reference'
query_indices = adata_combined.obs['dataset'] == 'query'

# Use scanpy's ingest for label transfer
adata_ref_subset = adata_combined[ref_indices].copy()
adata_query_subset = adata_combined[query_indices].copy()

# Compute embedding for reference
sc.pp.neighbors(adata_ref_subset, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=50)
sc.tl.umap(adata_ref_subset)

# Map query to reference
sc.tl.ingest(adata_query_subset, adata_ref_subset, obs='celltype')

# Transfer predicted labels back to original adata
adata.obs['predicted_celltype'] = adata_query_subset.obs['celltype'].values
adata.obsm['X_umap'] = adata_query_subset.obsm['X_umap']

print("Cell type annotation completed!")
print("Predicted cell types distribution:")
print(adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].value_counts())

# Identify tumor and immune cell types
tumor_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if 'tumor' in ct.lower() or 'cancer' in ct.lower() or 'malignant' in ct.lower()]
immune_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if any(keyword in ct.lower() for keyword in ['t cell', 'b cell', 'nk', 'macrophage', 'dendritic', 'neutrophil', 'immune'])]

print(f"Identified tumor cell types: {tumor_types}")
print(f"Identified immune cell types: {immune_types}")

# If no explicit tumor/immune labels, use broader categories
if len(tumor_types) == 0:
    # Look for epithelial cells as potential tumor cells
    tumor_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if 'epithelial' in ct.lower()]

if len(immune_types) == 0:
    # Look for broader immune cell categories
    immune_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if any(keyword in ct.lower() for keyword in ['lymphocyte', 'monocyte', 'leukocyte'])]

print(f"Final tumor cell types: {tumor_types}")
print(f"Final immune cell types: {immune_types}")

# Create simplified cell type categories for CCC analysis
adata.obs['cell_category'] = 'Other'
for ct in tumor_types:
    adata.obs.loc[adata.obs['predicted_celltype'] == ct, 'cell_category'] = 'Tumor'
for ct in immune_types:
    adata.obs.loc[adata.obs['predicted_celltype'] == ct, 'cell_category'] = 'Immune'

print("Cell category distribution:")
print(adata.obs['cell_category'].value_counts())

# Perform cell-cell communication analysis using LIANA
print("Performing cell-cell communication analysis...")

# Prepare data for LIANA - need to use raw counts
adata_ccc = adata.raw.to_adata()
adata_ccc.obs = adata.obs.copy()

# Run LIANA
li.mt.rank_aggregate.by_sample(
    adata_ccc,
    groupby='predicted_celltype',
    resource_name='consensus',
    expr_prop=0.1,
    min_cells=10,
    aggregate_method='rra',
    return_all_lrs=False,
    n_perms=100,
    seed=42,
    verbose=True
)

print("LIANA analysis completed!")

# Focus on tumor-immune interactions
if 'liana_res' in adata_ccc.uns:
    liana_results = adata_ccc.uns['liana_res']
    
    # Filter for tumor-immune interactions
    tumor_immune_interactions = liana_results[
        ((liana_results['source'].isin(tumor_types)) & (liana_results['target'].isin(immune_types))) |
        ((liana_results['source'].isin(immune_types)) & (liana_results['target'].isin(tumor_types)))
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"Found {len(tumor_immune_interactions)} tumor-immune interactions")
    
    # Get top significant interactions
    if len(tumor_immune_interactions) > 0:
        top_interactions = tumor_immune_interactions.nsmallest(20, 'rank_aggregate')
        print("\nTop 20 tumor-immune cell interactions:")
        print(top_interactions[['source', 'target', 'ligand', 'receptor', 'rank_aggregate']].to_string())
        
        # Store results in adata
        adata.uns['tumor_immune_interactions'] = tumor_immune_interactions
        adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions'] = top_interactions
    else:
        print("No significant tumor-immune interactions found")

# Add spatial coordinates to obsm if not present
if 'spatial' not in adata.obsm:
    adata.obsm['spatial'] = adata.obs[['x', 'y']].values

# Create visualization
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 12))

# Plot 1: Spatial distribution of cell types
sc.pl.embedding(adata, basis='spatial', color='predicted_celltype', ax=axes[0,0], 
                title='Spatial Distribution of Cell Types', show=False, legend_loc='right margin')

# Plot 2: Spatial distribution of cell categories
sc.pl.embedding(adata, basis='spatial', color='cell_category', ax=axes[0,1], 
                title='Tumor vs Immune vs Other Cells', show=False)

# Plot 3: UMAP colored by cell type
sc.pl.umap(adata, color='predicted_celltype', ax=axes[1,0], 
           title='UMAP - Cell Types', show=False, legend_loc='right margin')

# Plot 4: UMAP colored by cell category
sc.pl.umap(adata, color='cell_category', ax=axes[1,1], 
           title='UMAP - Cell Categories', show=False)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q33/run_0/cell_annotation_and_communication.pdf', 
            dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

# Save the results
print("Saving results...")
adata.write('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q33/run_0/output.h5ad')

# Print summary
print("\n" + "="*50)
print("ANALYSIS SUMMARY")
print("="*50)
print(f"Dataset shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Number of cells annotated: {adata.n_obs}")
print(f"Number of unique cell types predicted: {adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].nunique()}")
print(f"Cell type distribution:")
for celltype, count in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].value_counts().head(10).items():
    print(f"  {celltype}: {count} cells ({count/adata.n_obs*100:.1f}%)")

print(f"\nCell category distribution:")
for category, count in adata.obs['cell_category'].value_counts().items():
    print(f"  {category}: {count} cells ({count/adata.n_obs*100:.1f}%)")

if 'tumor_immune_interactions' in adata.uns:
    print(f"\nCell-cell communication analysis:")
    print(f"  Total tumor-immune interactions detected: {len(adata.uns['tumor_immune_interactions'])}")
    print(f"  Top interactions saved in adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions']")
    
    if len(adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions']) > 0:
        top_interaction = adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions'].iloc[0]
        print(f"  Most significant interaction: {top_interaction['source']} -> {top_interaction['target']} "
              f"({top_interaction['ligand']}-{top_interaction['receptor']})")

print(f"\nResults saved to: /Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q33/run_0/output.h5ad")
print("Analysis completed successfully!")
```

import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import anndata as AnnData
import liana as li
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Set up scanpy settings
sc.settings.verbosity = 3
sc.settings.set_figure_params(dpi=80, facecolor='white')

# Load the spatial transcriptomics data
print("Loading spatial transcriptomics data...")
adata = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/breast_cancer/breast_cancer_no_celltype.h5ad')
print(f"Loaded data shape: {adata.shape}")

# Load reference data
print("Loading reference data...")
ref_adata = sc.read_h5ad('./breast_cancer_reference.h5ad')

# Make variable names unique
adata.var_names_make_unique()
ref_adata.var_names_make_unique()

# Find common genes between datasets
common_genes = adata.var_names.intersection(ref_adata.var_names)
print(f"Number of common genes: {len(common_genes)}")

# Subset both datasets to common genes
adata = adata[:, common_genes].copy()
ref_adata = ref_adata[:, common_genes].copy()

sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(adata)
adata.raw = adata
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5)
adata = adata[:, adata.var.highly_variable]

ref_adata.raw = ref_adata
sc.pp.normalize_total(ref_adata, target_sum=1e4)
sc.pp.log1p(ref_adata)

# Find highly variable genes in reference
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(ref_adata, min_mean=0.0125, max_mean=3, min_disp=0.5)

# Use intersection of highly variable genes
hvg_intersection = adata.var_names.intersection(ref_adata.var[ref_adata.var.highly_variable].index)
print(f"Number of highly variable genes for integration: {len(hvg_intersection)}")

adata = adata[:, hvg_intersection].copy()
ref_adata = ref_adata[:, hvg_intersection].copy()

# Scale data
sc.pp.scale(adata, max_value=10)
sc.pp.scale(ref_adata, max_value=10)

# PCA
sc.tl.pca(adata, svd_solver='arpack')
sc.tl.pca(ref_adata, svd_solver='arpack')

# Concatenate datasets for label transfer
print("Preparing data for label transfer...")
ref_adata.obs['batch'] = 'reference'
adata.obs['batch'] = 'query'
ref_adata.obs['celltype_original'] = ref_adata.obs['celltype'].copy()

# Create combined dataset
adata_combined = AnnData.concatenate(ref_adata, adata, batch_key='dataset', batch_categories=['reference', 'query'])

# Compute neighbors and UMAP for combined dataset
print("Computing neighborhood graph...")
sc.pp.neighbors(adata_combined, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=50)
sc.tl.umap(adata_combined)

# Perform label transfer using ingest
print("Performing label transfer...")
# Split back
ref_indices = adata_combined.obs['dataset'] == 'reference'
query_indices = adata_combined.obs['dataset'] == 'query'

# Use scanpy's ingest for label transfer
adata_ref_subset = adata_combined[ref_indices].copy()
adata_query_subset = adata_combined[query_indices].copy()

# Compute embedding for reference
sc.pp.neighbors(adata_ref_subset, n_neighbors=15, n_pcs=50)
sc.tl.umap(adata_ref_subset)

# Map query to reference
sc.tl.ingest(adata_query_subset, adata_ref_subset, obs='celltype')

# Transfer predicted labels back to original adata
adata.obs['predicted_celltype'] = adata_query_subset.obs['celltype'].values
adata.obsm['X_umap'] = adata_query_subset.obsm['X_umap']

print("Cell type annotation completed!")
print("Predicted cell types distribution:")
print(adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].value_counts())

# Identify tumor and immune cell types
tumor_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if 'tumor' in ct.lower() or 'cancer' in ct.lower() or 'malignant' in ct.lower()]
immune_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if any(keyword in ct.lower() for keyword in ['t cell', 'b cell', 'nk', 'macrophage', 'dendritic', 'neutrophil', 'immune'])]

print(f"Identified tumor cell types: {tumor_types}")
print(f"Identified immune cell types: {immune_types}")

if len(tumor_types) == 0:
    # Look for epithelial cells as potential tumor cells
    tumor_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if 'epithelial' in ct.lower()]

if len(immune_types) == 0:
    # Look for broader immune cell categories
    immune_types = [ct for ct in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].unique() if any(keyword in ct.lower() for keyword in ['lymphocyte', 'monocyte', 'leukocyte'])]

print(f"Final tumor cell types: {tumor_types}")
print(f"Final immune cell types: {immune_types}")

# Create simplified cell type categories for CCC analysis
adata.obs['cell_category'] = 'Other'
for ct in tumor_types:
    adata.obs.loc[adata.obs['predicted_celltype'] == ct, 'cell_category'] = 'Tumor'
for ct in immune_types:
    adata.obs.loc[adata.obs['predicted_celltype'] == ct, 'cell_category'] = 'Immune'

print("Cell category distribution:")
print(adata.obs['cell_category'].value_counts())

# Perform cell-cell communication analysis using LIANA
print("Performing cell-cell communication analysis...")

# Prepare data for LIANA - need to use raw counts
adata_ccc = adata.raw.to_adata()
adata_ccc.obs = adata.obs.copy()

# Run LIANA
li.mt.rank_aggregate.by_sample(
    adata_ccc,
    groupby='predicted_celltype',
    resource_name='consensus',
    expr_prop=0.1,
    min_cells=10,
    aggregate_method='rra',
    return_all_lrs=False,
    n_perms=100,
    seed=42,
    verbose=True
)

print("LIANA analysis completed!")

# Focus on tumor-immune interactions
if 'liana_res' in adata_ccc.uns:
    liana_results = adata_ccc.uns['liana_res']
    
    # Filter for tumor-immune interactions
    tumor_immune_interactions = liana_results[
        ((liana_results['source'].isin(tumor_types)) & (liana_results['target'].isin(immune_types))) |
        ((liana_results['source'].isin(immune_types)) & (liana_results['target'].isin(tumor_types)))
    ].copy()
    
    print(f"Found {len(tumor_immune_interactions)} tumor-immune interactions")
    
    # Get top significant interactions
    if len(tumor_immune_interactions) > 0:
        top_interactions = tumor_immune_interactions.nsmallest(20, 'rank_aggregate')
        print("\nTop 20 tumor-immune cell interactions:")
        print(top_interactions[['source', 'target', 'ligand', 'receptor', 'rank_aggregate']].to_string())
        
        # Store results in adata
        adata.uns['tumor_immune_interactions'] = tumor_immune_interactions
        adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions'] = top_interactions
    else:
        print("No significant tumor-immune interactions found")

# Add spatial coordinates to obsm if not present
if 'spatial' not in adata.obsm:
    adata.obsm['spatial'] = adata.obs[['x', 'y']].values

# Create visualization
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 12))

# Plot 1: Spatial distribution of cell types
sc.pl.embedding(adata, basis='spatial', color='predicted_celltype', ax=axes[0,0], 
                title='Spatial Distribution of Cell Types', show=False, legend_loc='right margin')

# Plot 2: Spatial distribution of cell categories
sc.pl.embedding(adata, basis='spatial', color='cell_category', ax=axes[0,1], 
                title='Tumor vs Immune vs Other Cells', show=False)

# Plot 3: UMAP colored by cell type
sc.pl.umap(adata, color='predicted_celltype', ax=axes[1,0], 
           title='UMAP - Cell Types', show=False, legend_loc='right margin')

# Plot 4: UMAP colored by cell category
sc.pl.umap(adata, color='cell_category', ax=axes[1,1], 
           title='UMAP - Cell Categories', show=False)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q33/run_0/cell_annotation_and_communication.pdf', 
            dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.close()

# Save the results
print("Saving results...")
adata.write('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q33/run_0/output.h5ad')

# Print summary
print("\n" + "="*50)
print("ANALYSIS SUMMARY")
print("="*50)
print(f"Dataset shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Number of cells annotated: {adata.n_obs}")
print(f"Number of unique cell types predicted: {adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].nunique()}")
print(f"Cell type distribution:")
for celltype, count in adata.obs['predicted_celltype'].value_counts().head(10).items():
    print(f"  {celltype}: {count} cells ({count/adata.n_obs*100:.1f}%)")

print(f"\nCell category distribution:")
for category, count in adata.obs['cell_category'].value_counts().items():
    print(f"  {category}: {count} cells ({count/adata.n_obs*100:.1f}%)")

if 'tumor_immune_interactions' in adata.uns:
    print(f"\nCell-cell communication analysis:")
    print(f"  Total tumor-immune interactions detected: {len(adata.uns['tumor_immune_interactions'])}")
    print(f"  Top interactions saved in adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions']")
    
    if len(adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions']) > 0:
        top_interaction = adata.uns['top_tumor_immune_interactions'].iloc[0]
        print(f"  Most significant interaction: {top_interaction['source']} -> {top_interaction['target']} "
              f"({top_interaction['ligand']}-{top_interaction['receptor']})")

print(f"\nResults saved to: /Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q33/run_0/output.h5ad")
print("Analysis completed successfully!")
